posts.title: Why Your Cross-Validation Is Probably Too Optimistic
posts.excerpt: Your cross-validation score is likely inflated by leakage you have not noticed. Here is how it happens and how to fix it.
posts.thumb_alt: Cover image for the article: Why Your Cross-Validation Is Probably Too Optimistic

posts.title: Label Leakage: The Subtle Kind That Survives Code Review
posts.excerpt: Obvious leakage gets caught. The subtle kind hides in feature engineering, joins, and preprocessing, and it survives code review.
posts.thumb_alt: Cover image for the article: Label Leakage: The Subtle Kind That Survives Code Review

posts.title: Precision-Recall Curves vs ROC Curves: When to Use Which
posts.excerpt: ROC curves flatter you on imbalanced data. PR curves tell the truth. Here's the intuition, a worked example, and a rule of thumb you can actually use.
posts.thumb_alt: Cover image for the article: Precision-Recall Curves vs ROC Curves: When to Use Which

posts.title: Why ROC-AUC Can Mislead on Imbalanced Datasets
posts.excerpt: ROC-AUC stays deceptively high even when a model is nearly useless at catching rare positives. Here is the intuition, a worked example, and what to use instead.
posts.thumb_alt: Cover image for the article: Why ROC-AUC Can Mislead on Imbalanced Datasets

posts.title: The Bias-Variance Tradeoff, Explained on One Dataset
posts.excerpt: One small dataset, three model complexities, and a resampling experiment that makes the bias-variance tradeoff impossible to misunderstand.
posts.thumb_alt: Cover image for the article: The Bias-Variance Tradeoff, Explained on One Dataset

posts.title: A tuned GRU beat LoRA-fine-tuned GPT-2, here's why
posts.excerpt: A 117M-parameter transformer lost to a small recurrent net on every metric. Not because transformers are bad, but because the baseline was done properly.

posts.title: How a naïve train/test split inflated my UAV F1 by 0.5
posts.excerpt: The same model, the same data, and a macro-F1 that fell from 0.78 to 0.26 the moment I split the data honestly. A walk through the most expensive bug in ML.
